s1_reasoning: The computational intensity (FLOPs/byte) is low due to repeated streaming of large matrices through memory. The inner loops access matrices with non-contiguous strides (B[k][j] and C[k][j] in column-major order), causing poor cache utilization and memory bandwidth saturation.

hotspots.reason: Triple nested loop performing matrix multiplication (NI x NJ x NK operations) with poor cache locality due to column-major access of B matrix
Triple nested loop performing matrix multiplication (NI x NL x NJ operations) with poor cache locality due to column-major access of C matrix
Simple initialization loops that are O(n²) but only run once

bottleneck_type.reasoning: The computational intensity (FLOPs/byte) is low due to repeated streaming of large matrices through memory. The inner loops access matrices with non-contiguous strides (B[k][j] and C[k][j] in column-major order), causing poor cache utilization and memory bandwidth saturation.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The computation is highly parallel (O(n³) operations on O(n²) data), which maps well to GPU architectures. Matrix multiplication is a classic GPU-accelerated operation with high arithmetic intensity when optimized.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use CUDA or OpenCL with tiled matrix multiplication algorithms. For the first stage: assign thread blocks to compute tiles of tmp, with each block using shared memory for A and B tiles. For the second stage: similarly compute D from tmp and C. Use libraries like cuBLAS for optimal performance.
